name: Roast Pork Tenderloin W/Peppers & Onions
recipeIngredient: 6 large red peppers, cored, seeded and cut lengthwise into 6 pieces
6 large green peppers, cored, seeded and cut lengthwise into 6 pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine pork, onions, garlic, olive oil, and thyme in a large bowl; toss to mix.
2. Add red and green peppers; toss to mix.
3. Cover and refrigerate 2 to 3 hours, stirring occasionally.
4. Let stand at room temperature for 30 minutes.
5. Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C).
6. Season meat and vegetables with salt and pepper.
7. Transfer just the vegetables and garlic to one large roasting pan or two 13-inch x 9-inch roasting pans, dividing equally.
8. Roast vegetables in preheated oven for 15 minutes.
9. Push vegetables to one side of the pan(s).
10. Add pork (one tenderloin per pan if using two pans). Continue to roast until pork juices run clear – about 20 to 25 minutes. Do not overcook.
11. Transfer meat and vegetables to a platter; keep warm.
12. Discard garlic.
13. If there are any browned bits in the roasting pan(s), place pan(s) over high heat, add a little chicken broth, and scrape up browned bits from the bottom.
14. Add remaining chicken broth, Worcestershire sauce, and mustard to a medium-sized saucepan. Boil for 2 minutes.
15. Whisk together remaining chicken broth and cornstarch in a small bowl until smooth with no lumps.
16. Slowly whisk cornstarch mixture into sauce. Boil 1 minute until thickened, whisking constantly.
17. Strain through sieve into a bowl; you should have about 3 cups.
18. Season with salt and pepper and additional mustard if desired.
19. To serve: Slice pork diagonally into thin medallions. Spoon sauce over and serve with vegetables.
